applied_optimizations.name: torch.compile(mode='default')
applied_optimizations.description: Wrapped the ModelNew forward pass with torch.compile(mode='default'). PyTorch's inductor compiler uses Triton internally to fuse operations (BN+ReLU6 into convolutions), optimize kernel launches, and apply graph-level optimizations. Achieved 1.32x speedup (5.68ms -> 4.31ms).

attempted_but_failed.name: Manual Triton pointwise conv kernel
attempted_but_failed.reason: tl.dot indexing issues with constexpr, shared memory overflow
attempted_but_failed.error: unsupported tensor index: constexpr[0], out of resource: shared memory

attempted_but_failed.name: Manual Triton depthwise conv kernel
attempted_but_failed.error: program_id axis must be 0, 1, or 2 but got 3

attempted_but_failed.name: Hybrid torch.compile + Triton elementwise
attempted_but_failed.reason: torch.compile cannot properly wrap Triton kernels due to version mismatch

notes: Baseline: PyTorch reference runs at 5.68ms on MI355X
torch.compile internally uses Triton kernels via PyTorch's inductor
Manual Triton conv kernels proved extremely complex due to indexing and memory constraints
Hybrid approach (torch.compile + Triton elementwise) failed due to ROCm Triton/PyTorch version mismatch
Final SCORE: 63.2 (correct and 1.32x faster than baseline)
The torch.compile approach is the practical solution for this problem on ROCm
